notes: Non-IST extension of the Pune golden (drik_panchang.json). DrikPanchang's choghadiya page IS headlessly GET-fetchable (unlike the POST-only guna-milan calculator, docs/COMPATIBILITY.md). Names below are engine spellings; DrikPanchang prints the Sanskrit -a forms (Chara=Chal, Labha=Labh, Amrita=Amrit, Kala=Kaal, Shubha=Shubh, Roga=Rog, Udvega=Udveg). Day+night name sequences and (where fetched) Rahu Kaal were verified EXACTLY against the fetch on three weekdays, both hemispheres, and two DST zones. Sunrise/sunset are the minute-rounded DrikPanchang display; the engine (upper-limb+refraction, Swiss) is asserted within tolerance. Non-IST rise/set is already gated tightly by crossval_window.py; choghadiya names/parts are pure weekday arithmetic, so non-IST correctness reduces to rise/set correctness.
